message:
feat(fusion): add lifecycle/resource telemetry emission to TwoTierSearcher

Wire the TwoTierSearcher search pipeline to emit full lifecycle and
resource telemetry events through the host adapter, providing end-to-end
observability of each search session's lifecycle.

Lifecycle emission at session boundaries:
- Emit SessionStart lifecycle hook + Started lifecycle telemetry event
  at the beginning of search()
- Emit SessionStop lifecycle hook + final lifecycle telemetry event at
  normal completion (Stopped/Info) and at error/cancellation exits
  (Degraded/Error or Degraded/Warn)
- Include uptime_ms (wall-clock duration since search start) and
  skip_reason in the final lifecycle event

Phase health telemetry after each search phase:
- New emit_phase_health_telemetry() emits HealthTick lifecycle hook,
  a Healthy lifecycle telemetry event, and a resource telemetry event
  after each completed phase (initial, refined, refinement-failed)
- Resource events use RuntimeMetricsCollector.emit_resource() with
  the configured collection_interval_ms

Event correlation chain:
- Track telemetry_last_event_id across all phases so each subsequent
  event references its predecessor via parent_event_id
- Initial, refined, and refinement-failed search events update the
  last_event_id when they emit successfully

New internal methods on TwoTierSearcher:
- emit_host_lifecycle_hook(): fire AdapterLifecycleEvent on adapter
- emit_lifecycle_telemetry(): construct and emit TelemetryEvent::Lifecycle
- emit_resource_telemetry(): construct and emit TelemetryEvent::Resource
- emit_phase_health_telemetry(): combined health tick + lifecycle + resource
- emit_session_stop_telemetry(): combined session stop hook + lifecycle

Test additions:
- host_adapter_receives_lifecycle_and_resource_events_with_runtime_hooks:
  verifies Started/Healthy/Stopped lifecycle events, resource event
  emission, RuntimeMetricsCollector snapshot, and SessionStart/
  HealthTick/SessionStop lifecycle hook ordering
- RecordingHostAdapter gains lifecycle_events() accessor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
